This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation."},"item_number":{"type":["number","string"],"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error."},"temporary_id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). 2026-03-27T21:49:20.7178616Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-27T21:49:06.907567684Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57557,\"title\":\"Typed LRO convenience methods generate Operation instead of Operation\",\"body\":\"## Bug\\n\\nThe C# emitter generates typed LRO convenience methods that declare `Operation` in their return type, but their bodies forward directly to the protocol method overload which returns `Operation` — causing **CS0029 compile errors**.\\n\\n### Affected Operations\\n\\nThis affects LRO operations where:\\n- The initial HTTP response has no body (e.g. 202 Accepted)\\n- The LRO has a typed polling result via `@pollingOperation`\\n- The generator decides to emit `Operation` as the convenience return type\\n\\nExamples from `Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring`:\\n- `Train` / `TrainAsync`\\n- `CancelTrainingJob` / `CancelTrainingJobAsync`\\n- `EvaluateModel` / `EvaluateModelAsync`\\n\\n### Root Cause\\n\\n`LroVisitor.UpdateConvenienceMethod()` correctly updates the return type to `Operation` but misses transforming the method body when the base generator produces a simple forward (no `FromValue` pattern). The signature says `Operation` but the body returns `Operation`.\\n\\n### Repro\\n\\nGenerate `Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring` with zero custom files:\\n\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\powershell\\ncd sdk/cognitivelanguage/Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring/src\\ndotnet build /t:GenerateCode\\ndotnet build  # 6 CS0029 errors\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\n### Error\\n\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\nCS0029: Cannot implicitly convert type \\u0026#39;Operation\\u0026#39; to \\u0026#39;Operation\\u0026#39;\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\n6 error sites across 3 operations (sync + async for each).\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57557\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"radhgupta\",\"id\":210443219,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/radhgupta\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/210443219?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"bug\"],\"assignees\":[\"radhgupta\"],\"milestone\":\"2026-04\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-27T21:42:26Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-27T21:43:57Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-03-27T21:49:20.7196936Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-27T21:49:07.072273587Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57557,\"title\":\"Typed LRO convenience methods generate Operation instead of Operation\",\"body\":\"## Bug\\n\\nThe C# emitter generates typed LRO convenience methods that declare `Operation` in their return type, but their bodies forward directly to the protocol method overload which returns `Operation` — causing **CS0029 compile errors**.\\n\\n### Affected Operations\\n\\nThis affects LRO operations where:\\n- The initial HTTP response has no body (e.g. 202 Accepted)\\n- The LRO has a typed polling result via `@pollingOperation`\\n- The generator decides to emit `Operation` as the convenience return type\\n\\nExamples from `Azure.AI.Language.Conversations.Authoring`:\\n- `Train` / `TrainAsync`\\n- `CancelTrainingJob` / `CancelTrainingJobAsync`\\n- `EvaluateModel` / `EvaluateModelAsync`\\n\\n### Root Cause\\n\\n`LroVisitor.UpdateConvenienceMethod()` correctly updates the return type to `Operation` but misses transforming the method body when the base generator produces a simple forward (no `FromValue` pattern).
